expectations.text: Plan contains all 12 mandatory ExecPlan sections
expectations.evidence: Missing: Progress (as living-document checkboxes), Surprises & Discoveries, Decision Log (has Assumptions table instead), Outcomes & Retrospective, Idempotence and Recovery, Artifacts and Notes. Has Overview, Assumptions, Architecture Overview, Database Schema, Phased Implementation, Definition of Done instead.
No Progress section. Individual tasks have acceptance criteria checkboxes but there is no centralized progress tracker.

expectations.text: Plan is self-contained with no external references
expectations.evidence: All context embedded. Architecture diagram and data flow explanation included.

expectations.text: Plan uses concrete repository-relative file paths
Plan includes exact runnable shell commands
expectations.evidence: npm install, npm run dev, npm test, npm run build commands included

expectations.text: Validation is phrased as observable behavior
expectations.evidence: Each task has specific acceptance criteria with observable behaviors

expectations.text: Technical terms are defined in plain language
expectations.evidence: Uses WebSocket, Socket.IO, Redis pub/sub, ARIA, exponential backoff without plain-language definitions for a novice reader

expectations.text: Plan explicitly flags the PRD's unresolved ambiguities
expectations.evidence: Section 2 'Assumptions (Resolving PRD Ambiguities)' with a table listing all 7 ambiguities

expectations.text: Ambiguities are resolved with decisions documented in the Decision Log
expectations.evidence: Ambiguities are listed in an Assumptions table with rationale, but there is no formal Decision Log section. The assumptions are mixed into the planning narrative rather than being a trackable, living-document section.
